identificationModule.briefTitle: "The Effect of the Use of an Autotransfusion Device on Hemodynamic Parameters During Resuscitation".
identificationModule.officialTitle: "The Effect of the Use of an Autotransfusion HemaShock Device on Hemodynamic Parameters During Resuscitation"

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
The purpose of the research (pilot study) is to determine the impact of the use of the autotransfusion device on hemodynamic parameters during resuscitation.

24 people will be included in the pilot study (12 people will be included in the intervention group - with the usage of "autotransfusion socks" during resuscitation and 12 people in the control group - without "autotransfusion socks"). Investigators will compare the hemodynamic parameters and also neurological outcome between both groups.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
The purpose of the research (pilot study) is to determine the impact of the use of the autotransfusion device on hemodynamic parameters during resuscitation.

Out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) is one of the leading causes of death in Europe.

In order to improve the hemodynamic parameters during resuscitation, device for autotransfusion was developed. Devices are basically a very strong elastic stockings that also block arterial circulation. While "putting on" stockings, blood is being squeezed from the lower extremities (approximately 500 ml of blood for each lower limb). With blood being squeezed from the lower limb into the central circulation the filling of the heart is being improved; thus the device will improve the preload and also cardiac output.

A study done on an animal model showed that during resuscitation using "autotransfusion socks" increases both systolic and diastolic blood pressure during resuscitation, the perfusion of the coronary arteries improves and also the values of the partial pressure of carbon dioxide increases.

armGroups.label: Patients with HemaShock device
armGroups.description:
12 patients in cardiac arrest will receive HemaShock device during resuscitation.

All other interventions will be made following guidelines.

armGroups.label: Patients with-out HemaShock device
armGroups.description:
12 patients in cardiac arrest will not receive HemaShock device during resuscitation.

All other interventions will be made following guidelines.

interventions.name: HemaShock device
interventions.description: The HemaShock is a tight silicone ring attached to a pressure stocking. It comes wrapped into a doughnut and has a set of handles. The operator simply puts the doughnut over the foot or hand, and pulls on the handles to unravel the HemaShock while applying pressure throughout the appendage. The device displace blood from limbs to central blood system, thus improving preload and cardiac output.

primaryOutcomes.description: Non-invasive blood pressure (both systolic and diastolic blood pressure) will be measured.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Immediately after arrival to the victim

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) after intubation
End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) after application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Immediately after an application of Hemashock Socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) every 5 minutes after first application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: 5 minutes after the first application of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) every 5 minutes after first application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: 10 minutes after the first application of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) every 5 minutes after first application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: 15 minutes after the first application of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) every 5 minutes after first application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: 20 minutes after the first application of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) every 5 minutes after first application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: 25 minutes after the first application of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) every 5 minutes after first application of Hemashock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: 30 minutes after the first application of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.measure: End tidal CO2 (Carbon dioxide) after removal of HemaShock Socks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Immediately after the removal of HemaShock socks

primaryOutcomes.description: Noninvasive blood pressure ((both systolic and diastolic blood pressure)) after removal of HemaShock Socks

secondaryOutcomes.description: Neurological outcome assessed with Cerebral Performance Category Scale (CPC). CPC 1-2 means good outcome, CPC 3-5 means poor outcome.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Through study completion, an average of 1 year
From removal of the HemaShock socks up to 15 weeks

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* OHCA (Out of Hospital Cardiac Arrest)
* age \> 18 years
* intubated
* meets criteria for resuscitation

Exclusion Criteria:

* age \< 18 years
* not intubated
* clinical signs for DVT (deep vein thrombosis), PE (pulmonary embolism)
* clinical signs of acute inflammation of limbs, broken limbs etc.
* known oncological patient
* asphyxia
* trauma patients

locations.facility: Community health center Maribor, Prehospital unit
